title: quercus × garlandensis
intro: Quercus × garlandensis, also known as the Garland Oak, is a hybrid plant of the Quercus robur and Quercus alba species, commonly found in North America. The Garland Oak has been used in herbal medicine due to its tannins and phenolic compounds, which possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Quercus × garlandensis include its ability to support cardiovascular health, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of the Garland Oak can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and capsules, often used to promote overall well-being and reduce oxidative stress.
